title: Issue with TLS Verification: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

I rebooted my Mac OSX Big Sur 11.6 today and now I am getting the following error when trying to pull images with podman:

Error: initializing source docker://python:3.8.1: pinging container registry registry-1.docker.io: Get "https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/": x509: certificate signed by unknown authority


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. brew install podman

2. podman machine start


3. podman machine init

4. podman pull python:3.8.1
Trying to pull docker.io/library/python:3.8.1...
Error: initializing source docker://python:3.8.1: pinging container registry registry-1.docker.io: Get "https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/": x509: certificate signed by unknown authority

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version
Client:
Version:      3.4.1
API Version:  3.4.1
Go Version:   go1.17.2
Built:        Tue Oct 19 17:14:42 2021
OS/Arch:      darwin/amd64

Server:
Version:      3.4.4
API Version:  3.4.4
Go Version:   go1.16.8
Built:        Wed Dec  8 16:45:07 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
  kernel: 5.15.18-200.fc35.x86_64
  linkmode: dynamic
  logDriver: journald
  memFree: 1647390720
  memTotal: 2061381632

    name: crun
    package: crun-1.4.2-1.fc35.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 1.4.2
      commit: f6fbc8f840df1a414f31a60953ae514fa497c748
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL
  os: linux

  security:
    apparmorEnabled: false
    capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: true
    seccompEnabled: true

  slirp4netns:

  swapFree: 0
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? 
I am using the podman available on brew.

Why did I start getting this error today? I have been using podman for months now without issue.

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @DanielViglione!

@baude can you take a look?

could you try this command ?
```
curl -v https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/
```
It turns out it was a corporate proxy issue. Keep up the good work on Podman guys! I am using it more and more as an alternative to Docker.

@zhangguanzhang: Closing this issue.

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/13330#issuecomment-1069819327):

>/close


Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
